ai_strategy_lead.name: Julie Nash
ai_strategy_lead.title: Senior Vice Provost for Academic Affairs; AI Task Force leadership

documents.title: Exploring Artificial Intelligence (AI) — UMass Lowell CELT Hub

ai_strategy.summary: UMass Lowell has not published a single institution-wide AI strategy document, but its posture is articulated across three pillars: (1) a Faculty Senate AI Task Force (formed Fall 2023) charged with reviewing academic integrity, piloting pedagogy, and surveying campus; (2) an IT-run Generative AI policy restricting use to vendor-approved platforms with university agreements; and (3) AICORE — the Center for AI Computing Research — as the interdisciplinary research hub across four colleges. Campus framing emphasizes 'AI as a tool to enhance, not replace, human capabilities.'

key_provisions: Only vendor-approved platforms with official UML data agreements may receive university-related information
Users must authenticate with UML credentials before using approved AI for university work
Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat is approved for all faculty, staff, and students
ChatGPT access for faculty/staff requires executive cabinet approval (not guaranteed)
Student use of generative AI on coursework without instructor permission is academic dishonesty (effective August 29, 2024)
Faculty are strongly recommended — but not required — to include an AI statement in syllabi
ai_policy.summary: UMass Lowell's AI posture is cautious and data-protection-first. The IT Generative AI policy limits use to platforms that have signed data agreements with the university, with Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat as the default approved tool (deployed September 24, 2025). The Faculty Senate amended the Academic Integrity Policy in August 2024 to treat unauthorized student AI use as misconduct, and CELT provides four sample syllabus frameworks ranging from strict prohibition to permissive/encouraging.

bodies.name: UMass Lowell AI Task Force
bodies.scope: Faculty Senate body reviewing AI's implications for academic programs and integrity; issued policy revisions, ran campus readiness survey, and distributed AI pedagogy mini-grants to faculty

bodies.name: Center for Excellence in Learning and Teaching (CELT) — Exploring AI
bodies.scope: Operational home for faculty-facing AI guidance, syllabus templates, academic integrity resources, and the faculty AI mini-grants program

bodies.name: Office of the CIO — Information Technology
bodies.scope: Owns the Generative AI acceptable-use policy, vendor-approval process, and enterprise deployment of Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat

bodies.name: Center for AI Computing Research (AICORE)
bodies.scope: Cross-college research center led by Anna Rumshisky (Director) and Ming Shao (Associate Director); organizes AI research across Sciences, Engineering, Business, and FAHSS

governance_structure.summary: Governance is distributed across four bodies: a faculty-led Task Force anchored in the Senate and CELT, the CIO's office for acceptable use and enterprise AI tools, and the AICORE research center for scholarly coordination. There is no single chief AI officer or executive AI council. Julie Nash (Senior Vice Provost) and Donna Mellen (Director of Academic Technology) are the most visible administrative conveners.

initiatives.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat campus-wide deployment
initiatives.description: UMass Lowell IT deployed Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat as the approved enterprise generative AI assistant for all students, faculty, and staff, included with the existing Microsoft 365 license. Users must authenticate with UML credentials to keep inputs inside the university's data agreement. The license covers the standalone Copilot Chat app, not Copilot embedded in Word/Excel.
initiatives.significance: Default-on generative AI access for an entire R1 campus via Microsoft's free Copilot Chat tier is the emerging cost-controlled path for public universities that want to beat shadow AI without paying per-seat Copilot licenses.

investment.funding_source: Existing Microsoft 365 licensing (Copilot Chat included tier)
investment.description: Bundled with current M365 agreement; no separate disclosed add-on cost for the Chat tier

collaborations.description: Delivered through UMass Lowell's Microsoft 365 enterprise license

tools_and_vendors.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat
tools_and_vendors.role: Primary enterprise generative AI chat assistant for students, faculty, and staff

initiatives.policy_implications: Authentication and vendor-agreement gating is the control mechanism that keeps UML data out of public model training.

initiatives.title: Generative AI acceptable-use policy (vendor-gated access)
initiatives.description: IT-owned policy that restricts entry of any university-related information to AI platforms holding an official data agreement with UMass Lowell. Copilot Chat is the standing approved tool; ChatGPT access for faculty/staff requires executive cabinet approval on a case-by-case basis. Non-compliance can result in loss of tool access.
initiatives.significance: A clear posture of 'approved tools only' is one of the bluntest but most effective levers public R1s have for addressing FERPA/HIPAA/research data risk at scale.

investment.description: Policy work funded through existing IT operations

initiatives.policy_implications: Establishes a vendor-allowlist as the default data-protection control rather than relying on user behavior.

initiatives.title: Academic Integrity Policy amendment: unauthorized AI use = misconduct
initiatives.description: The Faculty Senate Executive Committee unanimously approved language on August 29, 2024 that classifies unauthorized use of generative AI tools — chatbots, image generators, code generators — as academic dishonesty. Prohibitions include entering exam or assignment prompts into AI tools, submitting AI-generated content as original work, and failing to disclose AI use when required.
initiatives.significance: One of the clearest Faculty Senate-approved AI-integrity amendments at a Massachusetts R1 public; sets a template for default-restrictive student policies with syllabus-level discretion.

investment.description: Policy amendment through Faculty Senate process

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates a disciplinary floor while permitting instructors to opt in to more permissive syllabus language through CELT templates.

initiatives.title: Faculty Senate AI Task Force (campus readiness survey + mini-grants)
initiatives.description: Convened Fall 2023 with faculty senators, administrators, and undergraduate/graduate students. Outputs: (1) the August 2024 academic integrity amendment, (2) an institution-wide AI readiness survey, (3) distribution of 35 AI Mini-grants to faculty incorporating AI into courses, shared through workshops and symposia.
initiatives.significance: A rare example of a Faculty-Senate-owned AI governance body (not provost- or CIO-owned) that produced both a policy amendment and funded pedagogical experimentation in its first year.

investment.description: 35 AI pedagogy mini-grants to faculty; specific dollar amounts per grant not disclosed

collaborations.description: Internal faculty governance and teaching center

initiatives.policy_implications: Faculty-led governance model counterbalances IT-driven AI policy and gives pedagogy a funded seat at the table.

initiatives.title: AICORE — Center for AI Computing Research
initiatives.description: Interdisciplinary research center coordinating AI work across the Kennedy College of Sciences, Francis College of Engineering, Manning School of Business, and the College of Fine Arts, Humanities, and Social Sciences. Directed by Anna Rumshisky; associate director Ming Shao. Core faculty (11) span CS, engineering, criminology, and business, with NSF CAREER and DARPA funding. Research thrusts include foundation models, safety/interpretability/alignment, adaptive learning, physics-informed AI, privacy-preserving ML, and human-AI interaction.
initiatives.significance: AICORE is UMass Lowell's bid to position itself among R1 AI research hubs without a massive standalone gift — a common mid-tier R1 pattern of cross-college coordination rather than a signature institute.

investment.funding_source: NSF, DARPA, industry grants to member faculty
investment.description: Aggregates externally funded AI research; specific endowment/operating budget not disclosed

collaborations.description: AICORE faculty participate in the UMass System 'Next: Frontiers in Artificial Intelligence' cross-campus initiative

initiatives.policy_implications: Research use-case exemptions are handled through grant-specific data agreements, not the IT Gen AI policy.

initiatives.title: NSF AI Institute for Collaborative Assistance and Responsive Interaction for Networked Groups (AI-CARING)
initiatives.description: UMass Lowell is a founding partner in the Georgia-Tech-led AI-CARING institute, a five-year, $20 million NSF AI Institute developing AI systems that support older adults (including those with mild cognitive impairment) and their caregivers. UMass Lowell's share is $2.4M, led by Distinguished University Professor Holly Yanco (Miner School Dean, NERVE Center director). UML's contributions: human-robot trust, evaluation methods, K-12 AI education, and DEI in the AI workforce.
initiatives.significance: Participation in an NSF AI Institute is the single strongest federal AI-research credential a university can hold; AI-CARING also cements UMass Lowell's national leadership in human-robot interaction.

investment.description: Five-year NSF AI Institute cooperative agreement across five universities plus industry partners

collaborations.description: Academic partners in the AI-CARING institute

initiatives.title: Manning Endowed Professor of AI + Helport AI Entrepreneurship Competition
initiatives.description: The Manning School of Business appointed Prof. Harry Zhu as the inaugural Manning Endowed Professor of AI (endowment funded by alum Brian Rist '77). Zhu launched the Manning School's inaugural AI Entrepreneurship Competition in 2025, co-hosted by Helport AI (NASDAQ: HPAI). Five student teams pitched AI ventures; 'Code Smart' (AI medical-coding prototype) won the $1,000 first prize. Honorable mentions: CoachHub AI (personal-training platform) and UML GradMate (advising chatbot).
initiatives.significance: Named AI chairs in business schools are still rare; pairing the chair with an industry-sponsored student competition is an early sign that AI pedagogy is moving past 'guest lecture' into sustained curricular infrastructure.

investment.funding_source: Rist endowment gift; Helport AI industry sponsorship of competition
investment.description: Endowed professorship + inaugural competition with $1,000 top prize

collaborations.description: Industry co-host of AI Entrepreneurship Competition

initiatives.title: Richard A. Miner School of Computer and Information Sciences
initiatives.description: In 2022, a $5 million gift from Android co-founder and triple UML alumnus Rich Miner — paired with a $2M state match — converted the computer science department into the Richard A. Miner School of Computer and Information Sciences. AI-focused leadership: Holly Yanco (Dean, robotics/HRI); Anna Rumshisky (Text Machine Lab, NLP/LLMs); Reza Ahmadzadeh (PeARL lab, autonomous systems).
initiatives.significance: Elevating a CS department to a named school with an AI-centered leadership slate signals institutional commitment to AI as a discipline, not just a research area.

investment.funding_source: Rich Miner gift ($5M) + Massachusetts state matching grant ($2M)
investment.description: Founding gift to elevate CS department to named school

collaborations.description: Philanthropic gift from Google/Android co-founder and UML alum

initiatives.title: Robot-manipulation benchmarking standards (NSF $1.5M)
initiatives.description: UMass Lowell researchers received a $1.5M NSF grant in 2024 to develop shared standards enabling roboticists to compare publicly available research on robot manipulation — directly tied to Holly Yanco's NERVE Center evaluation methodology.
initiatives.significance: Shared evaluation standards are a precondition for trustworthy deployment of physical AI systems; federal investment here supports the national robotics ecosystem, not just UML.

investment.description: $1.5M NSF grant for robot-manipulation comparison standards

initiatives.title: Online AI Automation and AI Essentials certificates (UMass Lowell Upskill + Ziplines Education)
initiatives.description: Division of Graduate, Online, and Professional Studies offers two stackable online certificates — 'AI Automation' and 'AI Essentials' — delivered in 5–10-week formats in partnership with Ziplines Education. Programs target working professionals seeking applied generative-AI and automation skills outside the traditional degree pathway.
initiatives.significance: Non-credit, industry-partnered upskilling programs are how mid-tier R1s monetize AI demand quickly without waiting for new degree approvals.

initiatives.title: B.S. in Data Science (proposed new degree — BHE 25-19)
initiatives.description: UMass Lowell submitted a Letter of Intent to the Massachusetts Board of Higher Education (BHE 25-19) for a new Bachelor of Science in Data Science, to be housed in the Miner School. Rationale: inbound demand from prospective students who want data-centric computing without the full CS curriculum. Curriculum spans 22 core courses, including new DATA-prefixed courses, and leverages the Miner School's existing Data Science Advisory Group.
initiatives.significance: A net-new data science B.S. at an R1 public is a visible commitment of faculty FTE to AI-adjacent undergraduate education — and a hedge against declining enrollment in traditional majors.

investment.funding_source: Institutional budget / MA Board of Higher Education approval

initiatives.title: MGHPCC / Unity cluster access for AI research
initiatives.description: UML faculty and postdocs access the Unity high-performance computing cluster, managed by the UMass Amherst Research Computing team at the Massachusetts Green High Performance Computing Center. Unity provides approximately 300 nodes, roughly 20,000 CPU cores, and approximately 1,500 GPUs for HPC/AI workloads. MGHPCC is shared across the UMass system plus BU, Harvard, MIT, Northeastern, and Yale.
initiatives.significance: Shared state-level HPC/AI compute is the model for most mid-tier R1 research universities; UML piggy-backs on MGHPCC rather than building its own GPU fabric — and the state's new AICR cluster will meaningfully expand that capacity.

investment.funding_source: UMass system consortium contributions + Massachusetts AI Hub investments

tools_and_vendors.name: Unity HPC cluster
tools_and_vendors.role: Shared GPU/CPU compute for AI research

initiatives.title: Massachusetts AI Hub participation (state-level)
initiatives.description: UMass Lowell is part of the UMass system participation in the Massachusetts AI Hub, announced by Governor Healey in 2024 via Executive Order 629 and backed by $100M in the Mass Leads Act plus a $31M state investment (May 2025) to build the Artificial Intelligence Compute Resources (AICR) cluster at MGHPCC. Projected joint state+university investment reaches ~$120M over five years.
initiatives.significance: State-level AI infrastructure investment creates compute capacity and convening power that no single R1 could afford individually; UML's proximity to Boston/Cambridge makes it a natural beneficiary.

investment.funding_source: Commonwealth of Massachusetts + MGHPCC member universities
investment.description: Five-year projected joint state/university investment in the MA AI Hub; UML's share is a subset

initiatives.policy_implications: Plugs UML researchers into a state-level governance and ethics conversation they would not lead on their own.

research_centers.focus: Robot evaluation, human-robot interaction, autonomous systems testing

research_centers.name: Text Machine Lab (Miner School)
research_centers.focus: NLP, deep learning, large language models

research_centers.name: Persistent Autonomy and Robot Learning (PeARL) Lab
research_centers.focus: Autonomous robotics, reinforcement learning, learning from demonstrations

MIST.6170 Advanced Machine Learning and AI (Manning School)

computing_infrastructure.summary: UMass Lowell does not operate its own dedicated AI supercomputer. Faculty and postdocs access the Unity cluster at the Massachusetts Green High Performance Computing Center — ~300 nodes, ~20,000 CPU cores, ~1,500 GPUs — shared across the UMass system and MGHPCC peers. The forthcoming state-funded AICR cluster will significantly expand capacity under the Massachusetts AI Hub umbrella.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: AI-CARING K-12 outreach and workforce DEI
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: UMass Lowell's NSF AI-CARING role includes developing K-12 AI/robotics education materials and initiatives to broaden participation in the AI workforce, managed through the NERVE Center.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Pell-eligible access baseline
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Nearly 40% of UMass Lowell students are Pell Grant-eligible; default campus-wide Copilot Chat access eliminates a cost barrier to a frontier AI assistant for this population.

regulatory_context.summary: Massachusetts is a high-engagement state on AI policy. Governor Healey signed Executive Order 629 (February 2024) creating the Artificial Intelligence Strategic Task Force, and the subsequent Mass Leads Act authorized $100M to stand up a Massachusetts AI Hub. The InnovateMA partnership (led with Northeastern) integrates universities into state AI adoption. At the UMass system level, the 'Next: Frontiers in Artificial Intelligence' initiative coordinates cross-campus AI research. UMass Lowell inherits and participates in both layers.

narrative_summary: University of Massachusetts-Lowell is a newly minted Carnegie R1 (February 2025) — research expenditures over $120M and nearly 150 doctorates annually — pursuing an AI posture that is cautious on policy and data protection but increasingly assertive on research and curriculum. Its governance is distributed rather than centralized: a Faculty Senate-convened AI Task Force (Fall 2023, chaired through CELT with Senior Vice Provost Julie Nash) owns pedagogy and integrity, the CIO's office (Steve Athanas) owns the Generative AI acceptable-use policy, and AICORE — the Center for AI Computing Research directed by Anna Rumshisky — coordinates research across four colleges. The university has not published a single strategic AI document and has no chief AI officer. Its policy stance is cautious: the IT Generative AI policy limits use to vendor-approved tools holding UML data agreements, with Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat deployed campus-wide in September 2025 as the default enterprise assistant and ChatGPT restricted to executive-cabinet-approved requests. In August 2024, the Faculty Senate amended the Academic Integrity Policy to treat unauthorized student AI use as misconduct. Research strength is concentrated in AI robotics and HRI, anchored by Distinguished University Professor Holly Yanco — dean of the Rich-Miner-endowed Miner School of Computer and Information Sciences (2022 founding gift: $5M + $2M state match), director of the NERVE Center, and co-lead of the $20M NSF AI-CARING institute with UML's $2.4M share. Anna Rumshisky's Text Machine Lab adds NLP/LLM depth with $1.4M+ in active NSF/NIH/ARO grants. Curriculum momentum: a newly endowed Manning School Professor of AI (Harry Zhu) launched UML's first AI Entrepreneurship Competition (with Helport AI), online AI certificates via Ziplines Education, and a proposed B.S. in Data Science (BHE 25-19, 2025). Infrastructure is leveraged rather than built — Unity HPC at MGHPCC provides ~1,500 GPUs, and UML benefits from Governor Healey's $100M Massachusetts AI Hub plus the state's $31M Artificial Intelligence Compute Resources (AICR) cluster. The biggest gaps: no published strategy, no chief AI officer, no dedicated AI compute, and relatively thin student-facing AI services beyond Copilot Chat. Overall posture: an R1 with credible AI research gravity (especially in embodied AI), a functional faculty-led governance model, and a quietly strong but strategy-light institutional response.

gaps_acknowledged: No published single institutional AI strategy document; AICORE founding date not stated on its public pages
Permanent provost search ongoing (on-campus finalist visits scheduled for April 2026); current title is Interim Provost (Noureddine Melikechi)
No named chief AI officer; AI strategy lead is de facto distributed across CIO, Senior Vice Provost Julie Nash, and AICORE Director Anna Rumshisky
Generative AI policy does not publicly list an explicit effective/adoption date; inferred as post-Copilot Chat rollout (September 2025) but likely preceding it
No publicly disclosed operating budget for AICORE or for the Miner School AI research portfolio beyond individual grants
UML does not operate dedicated on-campus AI compute; GPU figures cited are shared MGHPCC Unity cluster totals, not UML-owned
Exact dollar value of the Brian Rist endowment for the Manning Endowed Professor of AI not publicly disclosed
No identified active AI-related litigation or controversy at UMass Lowell as of the compilation date
